title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · Contract Lens: Js Migration
summary: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts js migration increment in a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Js Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 5.

content.title: Reasoning move

Apply that move specifically to Js Migration while producing a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio.
